rag_rules: Assumptions and guarantees must be placed in valid component type scopes; implementation-level bindings belong in implementation scopes.
AGREE expressions must preserve type consistency and avoid unresolved references.
Generated AGREE code should not introduce package-qualified identifiers not present in the model.
generic_rules: Fix root unresolved references before adding new AGREE clauses.
Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE annexes.
Move invalid implementation-level guarantees to the correct implementation scope or remove them.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Constants and equations in AGREE must use valid scalar types: int, real, bool.
Avoid referencing AADL properties like Period, Deadline, Priority directly in AGREE expressions.
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantee statements in component implementation scopes; they are only allowed in component types.
Do not reference AADL property names as AGREE variables without explicit model exposure.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about property associations
Model fusion and owner/scope preservation rules
